The washrooms in this college are in terrible condition. All toilet stalls in girls washroom of D block have broken flush handles - you have to manually pour water or leave it. Floor is perpetually wet creating a slipping hazard. There are no soap dispensers. Cleaning is done only once in the morning, after which condition deteriorates rapidly. By afternoon it is genuinely unbearable. This is a health and hygiene issue that directly affects girl students every single day. Please set up a proper cleaning schedule.

Right now there is none. Students give up after first complaint because they know nothing will happen.

Please take this seriously and hire a proper nutrition-focused caterer.

My issue is with the complete lack of academic resources for the Robotics minor introduced by our department. Robotics is a field that fundamentally depends on both hardware and software, yet our “college” has reduced it to just theoretical or software-based learning, which defeats the entire purpose of the course.
There are no basic resources provided  not even something as simple as an Arduino board for hands-on practice. I haven’t been shown a single working robot, nor has there been any proper demonstration of real robotics applications. How are students expected to understand robotics without ever interacting with actual hardware?
Despite this, the department still expects 75% attendance. On what basis? Why should students attend classes that offer no practical value or real learning experience?
If the college is not capable of providing even the most basic infrastructure required for robotics education, then offering this course is misleading and pointless. Institutions like IITs carefully design their labs and even involve experts or interns to decide what experiments students should perform. In contrast, our “beautiful college” is teaching only software like MATLAB in a so-called robotics lab.

This is not robotics education  this is a complete misrepresentation of the subject.

I am a 3rd year CE student and honestly the academic pressure here is unbearable. We have monday tests every week, ISA exams, practicals, and on top of that tons of assignments with declaration pages for each one. Where is the time to actually learn something? I want to build projects, prepare for interviews, learn new technologies  but the college keeps us so busy with pointless tasks that there's no time left. The professors themselves admit that half the syllabus won't be used in real life. Then why are we being evaluated on it? Please reconsider the academic structure.

Yaar campus ki cleanliness ki situation kya bata bhai. Canteen ke bahar ka area garbage dump ban gaya hai. Stairs ke corners pe kachra, dustbins overflow ho rahe hai, aur bahar ka road bhi littered hai. Students bhi zimmedaar hai for sure - but cleaning staff bhi visible nahi hai campus pe during the day. Koi ek person responsible hona chahiye jo ensure kare ki campus clean hai throughout the day. Ek Sunday campus cleanliness drive organize karo - involve students bhi. Kuch karo please.

The condition of girls' washrooms across all blocks is extremely poor. Door locks are broken in most stalls. We either have to wait for the one working stall or ask someone to hold the door from outside  which is embarrassing and time-consuming. During break time, there's a huge queue for just one functional washroom. Additionally, there's no proper liquid soap dispenser  just a random sprite bottle with a hole. This is a basic hygiene necessity. Please get the locks repaired and install proper dispensers urgently.
Benches in the canteen outdoor area are broken. Students sitting on them risk getting hurt. Replace them please.
During rains, water leaks into D block library area. Books and bags get wet. Building maintenance clearly not happening.
College events run way over time without warning. If event says 6pm end, it runs till 9pm. Students miss buses and trains because of this.
Fee payment portal crashes near deadline. Then late fee charged even when fault was portal's. Unfair.
Some professors teach entirely in Marathi. Students from other states or non-Marathi backgrounds cannot follow anything. English ya Hindi mein padhaana chahiye.
khana oily bahut hai
Computer lab booking is manual - go physically to check availability. Online system chahiye.
No proper first aid room or medical facility on campus. If a student falls ill or gets injured, there's no immediate help available.

College mein koi proper mentorship program nahi hai. No alumni network, no industry connect, no career counseling. When placement season starts, students panic because they have no guidance. Some professors who teach placement related subjects don't even know what companies ask in interviews. Meanwhile fees increase every year. What are we paying for? Just a certificate? We deserve actual career support.
